use_case_evidence.must_contain: BMad provides two testing paths: a built-in QA workflow for fast test generation and an installable Test Architect module for enterprise-grade test strategy.

phases.why: The testing reference page defines both the built-in QA path and the optional TEA path.

actions.why: The built-in QA path ends by executing generated tests and fixing failures.

evidence.must_contain: 5. **Run and verify** -- executes the generated tests and fixes failures immediately.

actions.why: The TEA path includes a dedicated test review workflow.

evidence.must_contain: | Test Review | Validate test quality and coverage against strategy |

actions.why: The TEA path includes an explicit release gate workflow.

evidence.must_contain: | Release Gate | Make data-driven go/no-go release decisions |

surfaces.why: The reference page is read as guidance and not rewritten during execution.
The page explains the workflow choices but does not define editable rule files.
The comparison page is about test automation and strategy, not normal production code edits.

evidence.must_contain: The QA workflow generates tests only.

surfaces.why: Both paths explicitly generate or automate tests.
The built-in QA workflow saves a test summary to project artifacts.

evidence.must_contain: The workflow produces a test summary saved to your project's implementation artifacts folder.

surfaces.why: The TEA path includes test strategy, ATDD, and traceability artifacts tied to requirements.

evidence.must_contain: | Test Design | Create a comprehensive test strategy tied to requirements |
| Traceability | Map tests back to requirements for audit and compliance |

surfaces.why: The TEA path includes explicit NFR assessment and release gate workflows.

evidence.must_contain: | NFR Assessment | Evaluate non-functional requirements (performance, security) |
| Release Gate | Make data-driven go/no-go release decisions |
If you later need test strategy, quality gates, or requirements traceability, install TEA alongside it.
Projects that have outgrown the built-in QA's single-workflow approach
